settings.hero_image_alt: Custom NZ native timber benchtop on a kitchen island

settings.heading: How do I measure for a timber benchtop?
settings.content: Start with overall length, depth and thickness. Then note overhangs, joins, end panels, sink or tap cut-outs and whether the top needs to work around walls or appliances. Rough dimensions are fine for a first quote; final dimensions need checking before cutting.

settings.heading: How do I choose the timber colour?
settings.content: Use the colour grid to compare Clear, Country Bark and Blackwash across Rimu, Tōtara and Beech. For a real-world decision, order timber samples and view them beside your flooring, cabinetry and wall colour.

settings.heading: Can I add sink and tap cut-outs?
settings.content: Yes. The configurator covers common sink and tap cut-outs. If the shape, join, overhang or cut-out is unusual, use the quote as a starting point and send through your plan or sketch.

settings.heading: How are the benchtops finished?
settings.content: Our standard finish is a hardwax oil that keeps the timber feeling natural and can be refreshed over time. It still needs sensible care: wipe spills, avoid standing water, use boards and trivets, and clean with suitable products.

settings.heading: Can I get a sample?
settings.content: Yes. Sample sets ship nationwide so you can see the timber in your space before committing. Order them here.

settings.heading: How long does it take?
settings.content: Lead time is confirmed in your quote - typically 4 to 8 weeks from order to delivery, depending on timber stock and your sizing. We'll tell you exactly when we come back to you.

settings.heading: Can I order an unusual shape?
settings.content: Most shapes work, including L-shapes, peninsulas, irregular angles and live-edge profiles. Use the configurator for the basic outline, and reply to your quote with anything bespoke. We'll work it out together.

settings.heading: Is the configurator quote final?
settings.content: No. It is an indicative quote to give you a serious starting point before we talk. We still confirm timber availability, final dimensions, joins, support, cut-outs, finish, delivery and any site details before anything is made.

settings.heading: Can you check my dimensions?
settings.content: Yes. Send the quote through with rough dimensions, photos, sketches or plans. We will come back with the practical questions and confirm the final dimensions before cutting.

settings.heading: What happens after I send the quote?
settings.content: It comes to our workshop team for review. We check the timber choice, size, finish, joins, cut-outs and delivery details, then reply with next steps. No payment is taken from the quote builder.
